title: [lichen_l2.c:1319 vs 1295-1306] peer_table cleared with memset but previous path uses secure_zero
description: Cross-file analysis: In lichen_l2_iface_init(), line 1319 uses memset() to clear peer_table, while the re-initialization path at lines 1295-1306 and the disable path in lichen_l2_enable() (lines 1071-1074) use secure_zero() for pubkeys. This inconsistency is intentional per the comment (C guarantees zero-init for statics), but creates a subtle issue: if lichen_l2_iface_init() is ever called a second time (after failed init followed by another init attempt), peer_table may contain stale pubkey material from the first attempt. Line 1337-1341 checks for this and rejects re-init, but if that check passes (lichen_iface was NULL), peer_table could have stale pubkeys from a partial link_ctx init. The memset at 1319 wipes them, but without secure_zero, the compiler could optimize it out. Should use secure_zero for defense-in-depth consistency.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed memset to secure_zero at line 1343 for defense-in-depth consistency with other peer_table clearing paths
